name: Coffee-Walnut Toffee
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a small cookie sheet.
2. Melt the butter in a heavy, 2-1/2 quart saucepan over low heat.
3. Add both sugars, water, molasses, espresso powder, cinnamon, and salt to the saucepan; stir until the sugar dissolves.
4. Attach a clip-on candy thermometer to the pan.
5. Increase the heat to medium. Cook until the thermometer registers 290°F, stirring slowly but constantly and scraping the bottom of the pan with a wooden spatula for about 20 minutes.
6. Remove the pan from the heat. Mix in 1-1/2 cups of nuts.
7. Immediately pour the mixture onto the prepared cookie sheet without scraping the pan. Tilt the sheet so that the toffee spreads to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
8. Sprinkle chocolate by generous tablespoons over the toffee, alternating bittersweet and white chocolates.
9. Let stand for 1 minute. Using the back of a spoon, swirl the chocolates to spread slightly.
10. Shake the sheet to form an even chocolate layer.
11. Using the tip of a knife, swirl the chocolates to create a marble pattern.
12. Sprinkle with any remaining nuts.
13. Refrigerate until the toffee is firm, about 1 hour. Break into pieces.
(Storage: Can be made up to 2 weeks ahead and chilled in an airtight container. Serve cold or at room temperature.)
